refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the load phase of the load→travel→dump cycle.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR combined the entire load→travel→dump cycle into a single requirement, which includes multiple distinct phases. To be atomic and solution-neutral, the FR is split into sub-FRs each addressing one phase of the cycle separately, avoiding conflation of multiple obligations.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the cycle time for the load phase under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, verifying that the p95 cycle time is less than or equal to the customer-defined target.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the load phase of the load→travel→dump cycle.
The NGHE solution shall support achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the travel phase of the load→travel→dump cycle.
The NGHE solution shall support achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the dump phase of the load→travel→dump cycle.

candidate_dps: Optimize load phase process sequencing and coordination
Implement adaptive control of load phase parameters based on real-time feedback
Allocate sufficient power and torque resources to meet cycle time targets
Design load phase operations to minimize idle and transition times
Incorporate predictive scheduling to anticipate and mitigate delays during load phase
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement adaptive control of load phase parameters based on real-time feedback

potential_sources: Dependency on accurate and timely sensor data
Integration with other cycle phases control systems
coupling_check.notes: Adaptive control logically isolates load phase timing adjustments, minimizing coupling with travel and dump phases while relying on feedback loops that can be modularized.

assumptions_risks: Real-time feedback data is reliable and available under site conditions
Control algorithms can respond quickly enough to influence cycle time
Operational policies allow dynamic adjustment of load phase parameters
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including feedback and control system requirements in the FR to clarify the need for real-time adaptivity and support DP selection.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for implementing adaptive control algorithms and real-time feedback processing for load phase parameters

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time feedback data and sends control status updates

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives commands and parameters for load phase control adjustments

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as adaptive control logic fits well within NGHE Solution; minimal diffusion as real-time feedback and control are core to this module.
Moderate containment; Vehicle module is broader and includes physical components, causing some diffusion of control logic outside software boundaries.
Low containment; Teleoperation focuses on remote control, not adaptive load phase control, leading to high diffusion.
Very low containment; Safety Services is focused on alerts and safety, unrelated to adaptive control, causing high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most specific and appropriate boundary to contain the adaptive control DP, as it centralizes the control algorithms and real-time feedback processing, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on real-time telemetry data availability and latency affecting control performance.
Integration complexity with Vehicle module for physical actuation of load phase parameters.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP directly addresses the FR by implementing adaptive control of load phase parameters based on real-time feedback, which supports achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the load phase. This mapping is logically sound and focused on the required functionality.

diffusion_risks: Integration complexity with Vehicle module for physical actuation of load phase parameters.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion and minimal diffusion for the adaptive control DP, as it centralizes control algorithms and real-time feedback processing. Other modules show higher diffusion scores, indicating that NGHE Solution is the most appropriate containment boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through measuring the cycle time p95 against the CT_target under agreed duty cycle and site conditions, as specified in acceptance criteria.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution module is clear and well justified, with explicit interfaces for telemetry and tasking that support the required functionality.

recommended_revisions: Explicitly address the integration complexity risk with the Vehicle module to ensure seamless physical actuation of load phase parameters.
Consider monitoring and mitigating potential latency and availability issues in real-time telemetry data to maintain control performance.
Document interface contracts and data exchange protocols between NGHE Solution and Vehicle modules to reduce integration risks.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with strong justification and clear allocation. The DP directly addresses the FR with adaptive control logic, and the IM (NGHE Solution module) is well contained with minimal diffusion. Verification feasibility is confirmed, and allocation clarity is explicit. Although integration complexity with the Vehicle module is noted as a risk, it is recommended to address this through risk mitigation and interface documentation rather than further decomposition. Given the high safety criticality, maintaining a clear and manageable design is paramount, and further decomposition at this stage is not warranted.

revise.why: Explicitly address integration complexity risk with the Vehicle module by documenting interface contracts and data exchange protocols, and consider monitoring telemetry latency and availability to maintain control performance.

assumptions: The adaptive control DP sufficiently captures the required functionality for the load phase cycle time target.
The NGHE Solution module is the appropriate containment boundary for the DP implementation.
open_risks: Integration complexity with Vehicle module for physical actuation of load phase parameters.
Potential latency and availability issues in real-time telemetry data affecting control performance.
evidence_needed: Documentation of interface contracts and data exchange protocols between NGHE Solution and Vehicle modules.
Verification data showing cycle time p95 meets CT_target under specified conditions.
